questions: How should library item resolution be implemented?
What module structure should the library commands use?
How should the CLI commands integrate with the main dispatcher?

answers.answer: Created resolve-library-item.ts adapting the resolve-ticket.ts 3-strategy pattern: (1) cuid detection via /^c[a-z0-9]{24}$/ regex for exact id match, (2) ticket shortId matching via case-insensitive comparison against item's ticket shortId (e.g. RSH-439), (3) title substring fallback with case-insensitive matching. extractLibraryItemRef extracts ref from --item flag or first positional arg.

Created new src/library/ module with nested router structure: index.ts dispatches to list/show/comments subcommands, comments.ts is a nested router dispatching to comments-list and comments-post. For comments subcommand, resolves library item first via resolveLibraryItem then delegates to runLibraryComments. Six files total in the module.
evidence: src/library/list.ts, show.ts, comments-list.ts, comments-post.ts: individual command implementations

answers.answer: Added 'library' case to main switch dispatcher in src/index.ts with configOrHelp pattern matching other authenticated commands. Added runLibrary import. Updated SKILL.md with 4 new commands in Available Commands table and Library Reports workflow section.
evidence: src/index.ts: library case with configOrHelp + runLibrary
skill-content/SKILL.md: 4 library commands and workflow examples

statement: All CLI Phase 2b changes implemented: resolve-library-item utility, 6-file library module with nested router, main dispatcher integration, and SKILL.md documentation. Build passes with zero errors. CLI library list command verified functional against dev server.
